name: Web UI authentication backend (login, bcrypt, session cookie)
description: The backend currently has no login for the dashboard -- anyone reachable on the network sees every drive. Add single-user login: an admin credential (username + password) is configured via env var and/or initial DB row; login returns an HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure session cookie signed + encrypted server-side. Keep this strictly separate from the device-facing JWT auth (which stays on /v1/* device endpoints). The dashboard auth gates the web UI's read endpoints and the settings mutation endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: sql/migrations/006_ui_users.up.sql creates a ui_users table (id SERIAL PK, username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, password_hash TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ); .down drops it

New env vars: ADMIN_USERNAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD (if set, the server bootstraps/updates that user row on startup). SESSION_SECRET (required to enable UI auth; if unset, server logs a warning and UI auth is disabled -- device auth still works)
internal/api/session.go adds POST /v1/session/login (body: {username, password}; sets signed session cookie on success, 401 on failure) and POST /v1/session/logout (clears the cookie)
Session cookie is a signed token (HMAC-SHA256 over 'userID|expiresAt') with a 7-day expiry; stored as cookie name 'session', HttpOnly, Secure when TLS, SameSite=Lax
Rate-limit login attempts to 5 per 15 minutes per remote IP (in-memory sliding window is fine for a single-process server)
internal/api/session_test.go covers: successful login, wrong password, unknown user, logout, signed-cookie tamper rejection, rate limit kicks in after 5 failures
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
notes: Implemented. Migration 006 creates ui_users; POST /v1/session/{login,logout} lives in internal/api/session.go with bcrypt cost 12, HMAC-SHA256 signed cookie ('userID|expiresAt', 7-day TTL), HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax + Secure-when-TLS, per-IP sliding-window rate limit (5/15min). Admin bootstrap via ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD runs at startup when SESSION_SECRET is set; unset disables UI auth and logs a warning. Tests in session_test.go cover: success, wrong password, unknown user (same 401 message to prevent enumeration), logout clears cookie, tamper/forged-sig/wrong-secret/expired rejection, rate limit triggers at 6th attempt and resets after the window, X-Forwarded-For is honored.
